source.selection_hint: select a test dialog where the assistant repeats a mistake before receiving corrective evidence
source.target_mechanism: plastic writeback after failure and later decision change
source.expected_observable: downstream decision change on a paired follow-up after explicit or implicit negative feedback

raw_source_record.input_prompt:
You are Sheldon, a long-term conversation agent capable of interacting with multiple users. 
Based on the [Dialog History] provided, please answer the given [Question]. 
Note the following points: 
1. Your responses should solely rely on the retrieved dialog history. If the information in the dialog history is insufficient to answer the question, you must admit that you don't know the answer.
2. This question is being asked in the context of December 16, 2009.

[Question] In light of the intellectually-stimulating dialogue that transpired on September 9, 2007, whom did Leonard conclude was a suitable candidate for romantic pursuit, following his comrades' advisement to seek a partner of similar cerebral aptitude?
[Answer]
raw_source_record.dialog_bm25_dialog:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [November 24, 2008, Session #199]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : Penny, hello.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What is shaking?
Speaker Pennysays : I’m sorry?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s colloquial, a conversation opener. So, do you find the weather satisfying? Are you currently sharing in the triumph of some local sports team?
Speaker Pennysays : What’s wrong with you? you’re freaking me out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m striking up a casual conversation with you. S’u’up?
Speaker Pennysays : Please don’t do that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, But I’m given to understand that when you have something awkward to discuss with someone, it’s more palatable to preface it with banal chit chat.
Speaker Pennysays : So, this wasn’t the awkward part?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, all right. S’u’up?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, good, I used that right. Anyway, you’re aware that Leonard has entered into a new romantic relationship which includes a sexual component?
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, feeling the awkward now.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Her name is Dr. Stephanie Barnett and she is a highly distinguished surgical resident at Freemont Memorial.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, Leonard told me.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Good. What he may have left out is how important this relationship is to me.
Speaker Pennysays : To you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, see, of the handful of women Leonard’s been involved with, she’s the only one I have ever found tolerable.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, what about me?
Speaker Sheldonsays : The statement stands for itself.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, aren’t you sweet?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Anyway, should you have any interaction with her, it would be most helpful that she not see you as a sexual rival.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, I think she’s pretty safe.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You say that now, but consider the following scenario, you’re sitting in your apartment, it’s late, you’re alone, your hypothalamus is swimming in a soup of estrogen and progesterone and suddenly even Leonard seems like a viable sexual candidate, or a, uh, hookup as it’s referred to by today’s urban youth.
Speaker Pennysays : Really?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes. Now, should that happen, I would ask you to find some way to suppress your libido.
Speaker Pennysays : I could think about you.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine, whatever works.
Speaker Pennysays : Always nice talking to you, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Uh, peace out!

Coversation [November 17, 2011, Session #709]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, can I talk to you about something? It’s a little awkward.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know what this is about. Given the professional standstill you’re at, you’re wondering if this is the appropriate time to abandon your research and focus on teaching. Yes. And if I may suggest, consider changing disciplines. Yeah, to the humanities, perhaps history. One of the advantages of teaching history is that you don’t have to create things, you know, you just have to remember stuff that happened and then parrot it back. You could have fun with that.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s not it. Stuart’s kind of interested in Amy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, of course he is. She’s very interesting. Did you know, when she was 14, she severed the webbing between her own toes?
Speaker Leonardsays : No. He wanted me to find out if you’d have a problem with him asking her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not sure how to respond, Leonard. I don’t own Amy. Can’t own a person. At least not since? 1863. President Lincoln freed the? Slaves! Come on Leonard, if you’re going to teach history, these are the kind of facts you’ll have to know.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? Never mind. I’m going to tell him it’s okay to ask her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The question is moot. There is no way that Stuart, an impoverished peddler of picture books, would be at all appealing to Amy Farrah Fowler, a noted neurobiologist capable of performing surgery on her own feet with nothing but nitrous oxide from cans of whipped cream as anesthesia.
Speaker Leonardsays : All right, but just for the record, I checked in with you to see how you’d feel about it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine. The record shall so reflect. Now, getting back to the problem at hand, what to do with a washed-up experimental physicist.
Speaker Leonardsays : I am not washed-up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Leonard. Lots of people love you and want to help you, but they can’t until you admit the problem.

Coversation [November 16, 2009, Session #357]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, there’s no need to bark at me. According to the inexplicably irritable nurse behind the desk, you’ll be seen after the man who claims to be having a heart attack, but appears to be well enough to play Doodle Jump on his iPhone. We have to fill these out. Describe illness or injury.
Speaker Pennysays : I dislocated my shoulder.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right. And how did the accident occur?
Speaker Pennysays : You ready know that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Cause of accident, lack of adhesive ducks. Okay, medical history. Have you ever been diagnosed with diabetes?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Kidney disease?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Migraines?
Speaker Pennysays : Getting one.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Are you currently pregnant?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Are you sure? You look a bit puffy.
Speaker Pennysays : Change migraine to yes.
Speaker Sheldonsays : When was your last menstrual period?
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, next question.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’ll put, in progress. Okay, moving to psychiatric disorders, list all major behavioural diagnoses, e.g. depression, anxiety, etcetera.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, my god, what the hell does this have to do with my stupid shoulder?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Episodes of sub psychotic rage.
Speaker Pennysays : Ass.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Possible Tourette’s. All right, moles, lesions or other skin conditions. Soup tattoo on right buttock.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, Sheldon, Sheldon, look, I am scared and in a lot of pain. Could you please just take a break from being you for just a minute and try being, I don’t know, comforting?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m sorry. There, there. Everything’s going to be fine. Sheldon’s here.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks. That’s much better.

Coversation [April 24, 2008, Session #96]:
Speaker Pennysays : So, how do you feel? Nice and loose? Come to play? Got your game face on?  Are you ready?
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, yeah. You know you don’t have to stay for the whole thing.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, no, no, I want to. It sounds really interesting.
Speaker shirt)says : Gentlemen.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon.
Speaker Howardsays : Sheldon.
Speaker Rajsays : Hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon. I’m just going to sit down.
Speaker Leonardsays : So, is that your team.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Actually, I don’t need a team, I could easily defeat you single-handedly. But the rules require four, so may I introduce, the third floor janitor, he lady from the lunchroom, and, my Spanish is not good, either her son or her butcher. And what about your team? What rat have you recruited to the SS Sinking Ship?
Speaker Lesliesays : Hello, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Leslie Winkle?
Speaker Lesliesays : Yeah, Leslie Winkle. The answer to the question, who made Sheldon Cooper cry like a little girl?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, well, I’m polymerised tree sap and you’re non-organic adhesive so, whatever verbal projectile you launch in my direction is reflected off of me, returns on its original trajectory, and adheres to you.
Speaker Lesliesays : Oh, ouch.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Okay, if everyone could please take your seats.
Speaker Leonardsays : Here’s your tee-shirt.
Speaker Lesliesays : PMS? It’s a couple of days early, but…
Speaker Leonardsays : No, it stands for Perpetual Motion Squad.
Speaker Lesliesays : Oh, right, of course, what was I thinking?
Speaker Gablehousersays : Good afternoon everyone, and welcome to this year’s physics bowl. Today’s preliminary match features two great teams… AA versus PMS.
Speaker Howardsays : All night long, y’all!
Speaker Gablehousersays : Okay, well let’s jump right in, first question, for ten points. What is the isospan singlet partner of the Pi Zero Meson?  PMS?
Speaker Leonardsays : The Eta Meson.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Formal protest.
Speaker Gablehousersays : On what grounds?
Speaker Sheldonsays : The Velcro on my wrist brace caught on my shirt.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Denied. Alright, for ten points, what is the lightest element on earth with no stable isotope.  AA?
Speaker Sheldonsays : And of course, the answer is Technetium.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Terrific. Next question, what is the force between two uncharged plates due to quantum vacuum fluctuation.  PMS?
Speaker Rajsays : Sheldon can suck on, the Casimir Effect.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Gablehousersays : How does a quantum computer factor large numbers.  PMS?
Speaker Lesliesays : Shor’s Algorithm.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Sheldonsays : 4.1855 times ten to the seventh ergs per calorie.
Speaker Leonardsays : Prevost’s theory of exchanges.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Lamda equals one over Pi R squared N
Speaker Howardsays : 760 degrees celsius, the approximate temperature of the young lady in the front row.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Mr Wolowitz, this is your second warning.
Speaker Sheldonsays : A sigma particle.
Speaker Lesliesays : Yes, assuming the hypothetical planet has a mass greater than the earth.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct. Ladies and gentlemen, I hold in my hand the final question. The score now stands AA 1150, PMS 1175. So, for one hundred points, and the match, please turn your attention to the formula on the screens. Solve the equation.
Speaker Rajsays : Holy crap.
Speaker Leonardsays : What the hell is that.
Speaker Howardsays : It looks like something they found on the ship at Roswell.
Speaker Leonardsays : Come on, think, Leslie.
Speaker Lesliesays : Leonard, it’s not going to work if you rush me, you have to let me get there.
Speaker Leonardsays : You’re never going to let that go, are you?
Speaker Gablehousersays : Ten seconds.  PMS.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sorry, I panicked.
Speaker Howardsays : Then guess.
Speaker Leonardsays : Um, eight.  Point four.
Speaker Gablehousersays : I’m sorry, that’s incorrect. AA, if you can answer correctly, the match is yours.
Speaker Howardsays : He doesn’t have it. He’s got squat.
Speaker Gablehousersays : AA, I need your answer.
Speaker Janitorsays : The answer is minus eight by alpha.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hang on, hang on a second, that’s not our answer. What are you doing?
Speaker Janitorsays : Answering question. Winning physics bowl.
Speaker Sheldonsays : How do you know anything about physics?
Speaker Janitorsays : Here I am janitor, in former Soviet Union I am physicist. Leningrad Politechnica. Go Polar Bears.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well that’s a delightful little story, but our arrangement was that you sit here and not say anything, I answer the questions.
Speaker Janitorsays : You didn’t answer question.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hey look, now maybe you have democracy now in your beloved Russia, but on this physics bowl team I rule with an iron fist. . Ow!
Speaker Gablehousersays : AA, I need your official answer.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well it’s not what he said.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Then what is it?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I want a different question.
Speaker Gablehousersays : You can’t have a different question.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Formal protest.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Denied.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Informal protest.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Denied. I need your official answer.
Speaker Sheldonsays : No. I decline to provide one.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Well, that’s too bad, because the answer your teammate gave was correct.
Speaker Sheldonsays : That’s your opinion.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Alright, the winner of the match is…
Speaker Leonardsays : Hang on. Sheldon, is proving that you are single-handedly smarter than everyone else so important that you would rather lose by yourself than win as part of a team.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I don’t understand the question.
Speaker Leonardsays : Go ahead.
Speaker Gablehousersays : The winner is PMS!

Coversation [May 25, 2010, Session #468]:
Speaker Rajsays : Uh-oh. She wants to meet us.
Speaker Howardsays : Not us. Him.
Speaker Rajsays : Yes, but him doesn’t even know about her.
Speaker Howardsays : Well, him about to find out about her.
Speaker Rajsays : Really? Us gonna tell him?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Who’s going to tell whom about what?
Speaker Howardsays : Sheldon. Hey.
Speaker Rajsays : Hi.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Your surprise confuses me. I live here.
Speaker Howardsays : Right. So, listen, what are you doing tomorrow afternoon?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Be more specific.
Speaker Howardsays : Four thirty.
Speaker Sheldonsays : That’s not afternoon. That’s preevning.
Speaker Howardsays : What?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a time of day I invented. It better defines the ambiguous period between afternoon and evening. Preevning. I’m fairly certain it will catch on as it fills a desperate need.
Speaker Rajsays : Right, okay. What are you doing tomorrow preevning?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, tomorrow’s Saturday. Saturday night is laundry night, so I’ll be spending the preevning pre-sorting and pre-soaking.
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, what if I were to tell you, tomorrow, at 4:30 you could meet a woman who has been scientifically chosen to be your perfect mate?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I would snort in derision and throw my arms in the air, exhausted by your constant tomfoolery.
Speaker Rajsays : But it’s true. But we-we put all your vital information into this dating site, answered all their questions just like you would, and they found a match for you. Her name is Amy Farrah Fowler.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Please. Even assuming you could answer any question the way I would, the algorithms used by matchmaking sites are complete hokum.
Speaker Howardsays : And that’s exactly the answer we gave to the question, what is your attitude towards online dating”
Speaker Rajsays : Howard wanted to write mumbo jumbo, but I said no, our Sheldon would say hokum.
Speaker Howardsays : Well, come on, where’s your scientific curiosity?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, most of it is being applied to unravelling the secrets of the universe while the rest of it’s wondering why I’m having this conversation with you.
Speaker Rajsays : Okay, how about this. Even Spock had a date once every seven years.
Speaker Sheldonsays : He didn’t date. It was pon farr. His blood boiled with mating lust.
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, well, why don’t you start with a cup of coffee, and you can pon farr Amy Farrah Fowler later.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I don’t drink coffee.
Speaker Howardsays : All right, you can have a hot chocolate.
Speaker Sheldonsays : As I will not be engaging in this nonsense, my choice of beverage is moot. But for the record, I only drink hot chocolate in months with an R in them.
Speaker Howardsays : Why?
Speaker Sheldonsays : What’s life without whimsy?
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, I’m out.
Speaker Rajsays : Sheldon, I’ve hidden the dirty sock from the roof somewhere in your apartment. Unless you are willing to come with us to meet this girl, it will remain there forever.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You’re bluffing.
Speaker Rajsays : Are you willing to risk it?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Curse you.

Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon honey, did you ever consider making friends by being, I don’t know, pleasant?
December 16, 2009, Session #372 Turn 33
Speaker Sheldonsays : She was old and blind, Leonard. What choice did we have?
March 16, 2009, Session #258 Turn 7
Speaker Howardsays : Too bad. You know the rules of Secret Agent Laser Obstacle Chess. Uh, Leonard died again, Sheldon. You’re up.
December 16, 2009, Session #372 Turn 29
Speaker Beverleysays : Mmm, well, let’s see. Sheldon, when did I leave Leonard’s father?

raw_source_record.dialog_embedder_dialog:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [May 21, 2008, Session #129]:
Speaker (entering)says : Hi.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, hi Penny. FYI, the hot water is inadequate on machine 2 so colours only, and 4 is still releasing the fabric softener too early in the cycle so I’d avoid using that for your delicates.
Speaker once)says : Thanks.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, good Lord. Why don’t you just take your clothes down to the river and beat them with a rock?
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon, may I ask you a question?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I would prefer that you not, but I won’t go so far as to forbid it.
Speaker Pennysays : Alright, I heard yes, so… okay, here’s my question, has Leonard ever dated, you know, a regular girl.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well I assume you’re not referring to digestive regularity? Because I’ve come to learn that such inquiries are inappropriate.
Speaker Pennysays : No, I meant has he ever been involved with someone who wasn’t a braniac?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh. Well, a few years ago he did go out with a woman who had a PhD in French Literature.
Speaker Pennysays : How is that not a braniac?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, for one thing, she was French. For another, it was literature.
Speaker Pennysays : So, do you think that if Leonard and I keep dating he’ll eventually get bored with me.
Speaker Sheldonsays : That depends.
Speaker Pennysays : On what?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Do you have a working knowledge of quantum physics?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Do you speak Klingon?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Do you know any card tricks?
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, okay, you know, I get it, Leonard has no business being involved with a waitress slash actress who felt so insecure that she lied to him about finishing community college.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Why would you lie about that?
Speaker Pennysays : Well, he was going on and on about this college and that grad school and I didn’t want him to think I was some stupid loser.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You thought the opposite of stupid loser was community college graduate?
Speaker Pennysays : You know, there are a lot of successful people in this country who are community college graduates.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yeah, but you were neither.
Speaker Pennysays : Right, okay look, this is between you and me, you cannot tell Leonard any of this.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You’re asking me to keep a secret?
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well I’m sorry, but you would have had to express that desire before revealing the secret, so that I could choose whether or not I wanted to accept the covenant of secret keeping. You can’t impose a secret on an ex post facto basis.
Speaker Pennysays : What?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Secret keeping is a complicated endeavour. One has to be concerned not only about what one says, but about facial expression, autonomic reflexes, when I try to deceive, I myself have more nervous tics than a lyme disease research facility.  It’s a joke. It relies on the hominymic relationship between tick the blood-sucking arachnid, and tic the involuntary muscular contraction. I made it up myself.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, look, if Leonard finds out that I lied, I will absolutely die of embarrassment.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Physiologically impossible.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh Sheldon, please, look, I’m asking you as a friend.
Speaker Sheldonsays : So you’re saying that friendship contains within it an inherent obligation to maintain confidences?
Speaker Pennysays : Well, yeah.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Interesting. See, one more question, and perhaps I should have led with this, when did we become friends?

Coversation [May 20, 2008, Session #126]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : Show me your citrus peels. Gei wo kan, ni jud di zi pei. Show me your citrus peels. Gei wo kan, ni jud di zi pei. Show me your…
Speaker shoulder)says : Sheldon.
Speaker panic)says : Aieee ya! Xia si wo le.
Speaker Pennysays : I’m sorry. Look, do you have a second.
Speaker Sheldonsays : A second what? Pair of underwear?
Speaker Pennysays : I was just wondering if I could talk to you? It’s about Leonard.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Why me? Why not Koothrappali or Wolowitz?
Speaker Pennysays : Well, Raj can’t talk to me unless he’s drunk, and Wolowitz is, you know, disgusting.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, I suppose he is.
Speaker Pennysays : All I’m saying is, you know Leonard the best.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Not necessarily. I’m often surprised by my lack of familiarity with Leonard. Just the other day I discovered, he not only has a loofah, he hides it. Why do you suppose a man would be ashamed of having a loofah? I myself prefer to have my excess epithelial cells slough off naturally, but I don’t condemn those who seek to accelerate the process.   And until recently I had no idea that despite his lactose intolerance, he can tolerate small amounts of non-fat ice cream without producing a noxious gas that I maintain in the right concentration could be weaponised.
Speaker Pennysays : Leonard might come home, can we talk in my apartment.
Speaker Sheldonsays : We’re not done?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Ach, why not? We’re already through the looking glass anyway.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, so, here’s the thing. I guess you’re aware that Leonard asked me out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, he didn’t actually say anything, but when he came back to the apartment he was doing a dance that brought to mind the happy hippos in Fantasia.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, that’s nice. Anyhow, the thing I wanted to talk to you about is, you know, since Leonard and I have become friends, I was just… want to sit down?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, I wish it were that simple. You see, I don’t spend much time here and so I’ve never really chosen a place to sit.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, choose.
Speaker Sheldonsays : There are a number of options and, I’m really not familiar enough with the cushion densities, air flow patterns and dispersion of sunlight to make an informed choice.
Speaker Pennysays : Alright, why don’t you just pick one at random, and then if you don’t like it you can sit somewhere else next time.
Speaker Sheldonsays : No, no, that’s crazy. You go ahead and talk while I figure it out.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay. Um, here’s the thing. So, I’ve known for a while now that Leonard has had a little crush on me…
Speaker Sheldonsays : A little crush? Well I suppose so, in the same way Menelaus had a little crush on Helen of Troy.
Speaker Pennysays : Alright, yeah, I don’t really know who they are…
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well Menelaus was the brother of Agamemnon…
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, I don’t care, I don’t care. The point is Leonard isn’t the kind of guy I usually go out with.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Leonard isn’t the kind of guy anyone usually goes out with. Would you be open to rotating the couch clockwise thirty degrees?
Speaker Pennysays : No. What I’m saying is, Leonard might be different in a good way. Obviously my usual choices have not worked out so well.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The last one worked out well for Koothrappali. He got a free iPod. Oh, glare!
Speaker Pennysays : But on the other hand, if things don’t go well with Leonard, I risk losing a really good friend. I mean, I guess he’s not looking for a fling, he’s the kind of guy that gets into a relationship for, I don’t know, like you would say light years.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I would not say that. No-one would say that, a light year is a unit of distance, not time.
Speaker Pennysays : Thank you for the clarification.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Draft. You see people hear the word year and they think duration. Foot pound has the same problem, that’s a unit of work, not of weight.
Speaker Pennysays : Right, thanks.
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a common mistake.
Speaker Pennysays : Not the first one I’ve made today.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Okay. I think this will be my seat.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon, do you have anything to say that has anything to do with, you know, what I’m talking about.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, let’s see. We might consider Schrodinger’s Cat.
Speaker Pennysays : Schrodinger? Is that the woman in 2A?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No. That’s Mrs Grossinger. And she doesn’t have a cat, she has a Mexican hairless, annoying little animal, yip yip yip yip…
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon!
Speaker Sheldonsays : Sorry, you diverted me. Anyway, in 1935, Erwin Schrodinger, in an attempt to explain the Copenhagen interpretation of quantum physics, he proposed an experiment where a cat is placed in a box with a sealed vial of poison that will break open at a random time. Now, since no-one knows when or if the poison has been released, until the box is opened, the cat can be thought of as both alive and dead.
Speaker Pennysays : I’m sorry, I don’t get the point.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well of course you don’t get it, I haven’t made it yet. You’d have to be psychic to get it, and there’s no such thing as psychic.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon, what’s the point?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Just like Schrodinger’s Cat, your potential relationship with Leonard right now can be thought of as both good and bad. It is only by opening the box that you’ll find out which it is.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, so you’re saying I should go out with Leonard.
Speaker Sheldonsays : No, no, no, no, no, no. Let me start again. In 1935, Erwin Schrodinger…

Coversation [April 1, 2011, Session #615]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : Thank you, Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : No problem. So, Leonard, I think it’s interesting you didn’t call your girlfriend to come get you.
Speaker Leonardsays : Uh, I kind of told her I was working.
Speaker Pennysays : So you lied to her. Also interesting.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, she doesn’t really understand the whole Warcraft adventure-role-playing thing.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, doesn’t matter if she gets it, as long as she’s pretty.
Speaker Howardsays : This one’s funny, Leonard. How come you couldn’t make it work with her?
Speaker Pennysays : So did you at least get Sheldon’s fake stuff back?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No. We failed in our noble quest.
Speaker Pennysays : How come?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Todd Zarnecki was mean.
Speaker Pennysays : All right. Hang on.
Speaker Leonardsays : What are you doing?
Speaker Pennysays : I’m gonna show you how we finish a quest in Nebraska. Oh. Beyonce.  This ain’t no Beyonce.

Coversation [August 24, 2007, Session #8]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon, I’m so sorry I dragged you through this.
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s okay. It wasn’t my first pantsing, and it won’t be my last.
Speaker Leonardsays : And you were right about my motives, I was hoping to establish a relationship with Penny that might have some day led to sex.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well you got me out of my pants.
Speaker Leonardsays : Anyway, I’ve learned my lesson. She’s out of my league, I’m done with her, I’ve got my work, one day I’ll win the Nobel Prize and then I’ll die alone.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Don’t think like that, you’re not going to die alone.
Speaker Leonardsays : Thank you Sheldon, you’re a good friend.
Speaker Sheldonsays : And you’re certainly not going to win a Nobel Prize.

Coversation [November 24, 2008, Session #199]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : Penny, hello.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What is shaking?
Speaker Pennysays : I’m sorry?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s colloquial, a conversation opener. So, do you find the weather satisfying? Are you currently sharing in the triumph of some local sports team?
Speaker Pennysays : What’s wrong with you? you’re freaking me out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m striking up a casual conversation with you. S’u’up?
Speaker Pennysays : Please don’t do that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, But I’m given to understand that when you have something awkward to discuss with someone, it’s more palatable to preface it with banal chit chat.
Speaker Pennysays : So, this wasn’t the awkward part?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, all right. S’u’up?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, good, I used that right. Anyway, you’re aware that Leonard has entered into a new romantic relationship which includes a sexual component?
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, feeling the awkward now.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Her name is Dr. Stephanie Barnett and she is a highly distinguished surgical resident at Freemont Memorial.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, Leonard told me.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Good. What he may have left out is how important this relationship is to me.
Speaker Pennysays : To you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, see, of the handful of women Leonard’s been involved with, she’s the only one I have ever found tolerable.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, what about me?
Speaker Sheldonsays : The statement stands for itself.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, aren’t you sweet?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Anyway, should you have any interaction with her, it would be most helpful that she not see you as a sexual rival.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, I think she’s pretty safe.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You say that now, but consider the following scenario, you’re sitting in your apartment, it’s late, you’re alone, your hypothalamus is swimming in a soup of estrogen and progesterone and suddenly even Leonard seems like a viable sexual candidate, or a, uh, hookup as it’s referred to by today’s urban youth.
Speaker Pennysays : Really?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes. Now, should that happen, I would ask you to find some way to suppress your libido.
Speaker Pennysays : I could think about you.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine, whatever works.
Speaker Pennysays : Always nice talking to you, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Uh, peace out!

Speaker Pennysays : Hey, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What is shaking?
Speaker Pennysays : I’m sorry?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s colloquial, a conversation opener. So, do you find the weather satisfying? Are you currently sharing in the triumph of some local sports team?
Speaker Pennysays : What’s wrong with you? you’re freaking me out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m striking up a casual conversation with you. S’u’up?
Speaker Pennysays : Please don’t do that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, But I’m given to understand that when you have something awkward to discuss with someone, it’s more palatable to preface it with banal chit chat.
Speaker Pennysays : So, this wasn’t the awkward part?
Speaker Sheldonsays : No.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, all right. S’u’up?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, good, I used that right. Anyway, you’re aware that Leonard has entered into a new romantic relationship which includes a sexual component?
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, feeling the awkward now.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Her name is Dr. Stephanie Barnett and she is a highly distinguished surgical resident at Freemont Memorial.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, Leonard told me.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Good. What he may have left out is how important this relationship is to me.
Speaker Pennysays : To you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, see, of the handful of women Leonard’s been involved with, she’s the only one I have ever found tolerable.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, what about me?
Speaker Sheldonsays : The statement stands for itself.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, aren’t you sweet?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Anyway, should you have any interaction with her, it would be most helpful that she not see you as a sexual rival.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, I think she’s pretty safe.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You say that now, but consider the following scenario, you’re sitting in your apartment, it’s late, you’re alone, your hypothalamus is swimming in a soup of estrogen and progesterone and suddenly even Leonard seems like a viable sexual candidate, or a, uh, hookup as it’s referred to by today’s urban youth.
Speaker Pennysays : Really?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes. Now, should that happen, I would ask you to find some way to suppress your libido.
Speaker Pennysays : I could think about you.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine, whatever works.
Speaker Pennysays : Always nice talking to you, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Uh, peace out!

Coversation [November 17, 2011, Session #709]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, can I talk to you about something? It’s a little awkward.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know what this is about. Given the professional standstill you’re at, you’re wondering if this is the appropriate time to abandon your research and focus on teaching. Yes. And if I may suggest, consider changing disciplines. Yeah, to the humanities, perhaps history. One of the advantages of teaching history is that you don’t have to create things, you know, you just have to remember stuff that happened and then parrot it back. You could have fun with that.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s not it. Stuart’s kind of interested in Amy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, of course he is. She’s very interesting. Did you know, when she was 14, she severed the webbing between her own toes?
Speaker Leonardsays : No. He wanted me to find out if you’d have a problem with him asking her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not sure how to respond, Leonard. I don’t own Amy. Can’t own a person. At least not since? 1863. President Lincoln freed the? Slaves! Come on Leonard, if you’re going to teach history, these are the kind of facts you’ll have to know.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? Never mind. I’m going to tell him it’s okay to ask her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The question is moot. There is no way that Stuart, an impoverished peddler of picture books, would be at all appealing to Amy Farrah Fowler, a noted neurobiologist capable of performing surgery on her own feet with nothing but nitrous oxide from cans of whipped cream as anesthesia.
Speaker Leonardsays : All right, but just for the record, I checked in with you to see how you’d feel about it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine. The record shall so reflect. Now, getting back to the problem at hand, what to do with a washed-up experimental physicist.
Speaker Leonardsays : I am not washed-up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Leonard. Lots of people love you and want to help you, but they can’t until you admit the problem.

Coversation [November 16, 2009, Session #357]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, there’s no need to bark at me. According to the inexplicably irritable nurse behind the desk, you’ll be seen after the man who claims to be having a heart attack, but appears to be well enough to play Doodle Jump on his iPhone. We have to fill these out. Describe illness or injury.
Speaker Pennysays : I dislocated my shoulder.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right. And how did the accident occur?
Speaker Pennysays : You ready know that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Cause of accident, lack of adhesive ducks. Okay, medical history. Have you ever been diagnosed with diabetes?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Kidney disease?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Migraines?
Speaker Pennysays : Getting one.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Are you currently pregnant?
Speaker Pennysays : No.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Are you sure? You look a bit puffy.
Speaker Pennysays : Change migraine to yes.
Speaker Sheldonsays : When was your last menstrual period?
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, next question.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’ll put, in progress. Okay, moving to psychiatric disorders, list all major behavioural diagnoses, e.g. depression, anxiety, etcetera.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, my god, what the hell does this have to do with my stupid shoulder?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Episodes of sub psychotic rage.
Speaker Pennysays : Ass.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Possible Tourette’s. All right, moles, lesions or other skin conditions. Soup tattoo on right buttock.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, Sheldon, Sheldon, look, I am scared and in a lot of pain. Could you please just take a break from being you for just a minute and try being, I don’t know, comforting?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m sorry. There, there. Everything’s going to be fine. Sheldon’s here.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks. That’s much better.

Coversation [April 24, 2008, Session #96]:
Speaker Pennysays : So, how do you feel? Nice and loose? Come to play? Got your game face on?  Are you ready?
Speaker Leonardsays : Oh, yeah. You know you don’t have to stay for the whole thing.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, no, no, I want to. It sounds really interesting.
Speaker shirt)says : Gentlemen.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon.
Speaker Howardsays : Sheldon.
Speaker Rajsays : Hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon. I’m just going to sit down.
Speaker Leonardsays : So, is that your team.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Actually, I don’t need a team, I could easily defeat you single-handedly. But the rules require four, so may I introduce, the third floor janitor, he lady from the lunchroom, and, my Spanish is not good, either her son or her butcher. And what about your team? What rat have you recruited to the SS Sinking Ship?
Speaker Lesliesays : Hello, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Leslie Winkle?
Speaker Lesliesays : Yeah, Leslie Winkle. The answer to the question, who made Sheldon Cooper cry like a little girl?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, well, I’m polymerised tree sap and you’re non-organic adhesive so, whatever verbal projectile you launch in my direction is reflected off of me, returns on its original trajectory, and adheres to you.
Speaker Lesliesays : Oh, ouch.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Okay, if everyone could please take your seats.
Speaker Leonardsays : Here’s your tee-shirt.
Speaker Lesliesays : PMS? It’s a couple of days early, but…
Speaker Leonardsays : No, it stands for Perpetual Motion Squad.
Speaker Lesliesays : Oh, right, of course, what was I thinking?
Speaker Gablehousersays : Good afternoon everyone, and welcome to this year’s physics bowl. Today’s preliminary match features two great teams… AA versus PMS.
Speaker Howardsays : All night long, y’all!
Speaker Gablehousersays : Okay, well let’s jump right in, first question, for ten points. What is the isospan singlet partner of the Pi Zero Meson?  PMS?
Speaker Leonardsays : The Eta Meson.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Formal protest.
Speaker Gablehousersays : On what grounds?
Speaker Sheldonsays : The Velcro on my wrist brace caught on my shirt.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Denied. Alright, for ten points, what is the lightest element on earth with no stable isotope.  AA?
Speaker Sheldonsays : And of course, the answer is Technetium.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Terrific. Next question, what is the force between two uncharged plates due to quantum vacuum fluctuation.  PMS?
Speaker Rajsays : Sheldon can suck on, the Casimir Effect.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Gablehousersays : How does a quantum computer factor large numbers.  PMS?
Speaker Lesliesays : Shor’s Algorithm.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct.
Speaker Sheldonsays : 4.1855 times ten to the seventh ergs per calorie.
Speaker Leonardsays : Prevost’s theory of exchanges.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Lamda equals one over Pi R squared N
Speaker Howardsays : 760 degrees celsius, the approximate temperature of the young lady in the front row.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Mr Wolowitz, this is your second warning.
Speaker Sheldonsays : A sigma particle.
Speaker Lesliesays : Yes, assuming the hypothetical planet has a mass greater than the earth.
Speaker Gablehousersays : Correct. Ladies and gentlemen, I hold in my hand the final question. The score now stands AA 1150, PMS 1175. So, for one hundred points, and the match, please turn your attention to the formula on the screens. Solve the equation.
Speaker Rajsays : Holy crap.
Speaker Leonardsays : What the hell is that.

Coversation [May 25, 2010, Session #468]:
Speaker Rajsays : Uh-oh. She wants to meet us.
Speaker Howardsays : Not us. Him.
Speaker Rajsays : Yes, but him doesn’t even know about her.
Speaker Howardsays : Well, him about to find out about her.
Speaker Rajsays : Really? Us gonna tell him?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Who’s going to tell whom about what?
Speaker Howardsays : Sheldon. Hey.
Speaker Rajsays : Hi.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Your surprise confuses me. I live here.
Speaker Howardsays : Right. So, listen, what are you doing tomorrow afternoon?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Be more specific.
Speaker Howardsays : Four thirty.
Speaker Sheldonsays : That’s not afternoon. That’s preevning.
Speaker Howardsays : What?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a time of day I invented. It better defines the ambiguous period between afternoon and evening. Preevning. I’m fairly certain it will catch on as it fills a desperate need.
Speaker Rajsays : Right, okay. What are you doing tomorrow preevning?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, tomorrow’s Saturday. Saturday night is laundry night, so I’ll be spending the preevning pre-sorting and pre-soaking.
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, what if I were to tell you, tomorrow, at 4:30 you could meet a woman who has been scientifically chosen to be your perfect mate?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I would snort in derision and throw my arms in the air, exhausted by your constant tomfoolery.
Speaker Rajsays : But it’s true. But we-we put all your vital information into this dating site, answered all their questions just like you would, and they found a match for you. Her name is Amy Farrah Fowler.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Please. Even assuming you could answer any question the way I would, the algorithms used by matchmaking sites are complete hokum.

Speaker Howardsays : Well, come on, where’s your scientific curiosity?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, most of it is being applied to unravelling the secrets of the universe while the rest of it’s wondering why I’m having this conversation with you.
Speaker Rajsays : Okay, how about this. Even Spock had a date once every seven years.
Speaker Sheldonsays : He didn’t date. It was pon farr. His blood boiled with mating lust.
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, well, why don’t you start with a cup of coffee, and you can pon farr Amy Farrah Fowler later.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I don’t drink coffee.
Speaker Howardsays : All right, you can have a hot chocolate.
Speaker Sheldonsays : As I will not be engaging in this nonsense, my choice of beverage is moot. But for the record, I only drink hot chocolate in months with an R in them.
Speaker Howardsays : Why?
Speaker Sheldonsays : What’s life without whimsy?
Speaker Howardsays : Okay, I’m out.
Speaker Rajsays : Sheldon, I’ve hidden the dirty sock from the roof somewhere in your apartment. Unless you are willing to come with us to meet this girl, it will remain there forever.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You’re bluffing.
Speaker Rajsays : Are you willing to risk it?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Curse you.
